text: From love comes grief, from love comes fear; he who is free from love knows neither grief nor fear.
author: (Pali Canon)
source: Project Gutenberg eBook #2017 (Dhammapada, trans. F. Max Müller; the same text as Sacred Books of the East v10, 1881)
out_pulls_note: High heat: the bald equation of love with grief and fear makes any stranger flinch before they can argue with it -- the craving->suffering core of Nirvana, felt not defined.
search_notes: F. Max Müller translation, 1881 (SBE v10); Gutenberg #2017 credits 'Translated from Pali by F. Max Muller'. Verbatim substring confirmed after whitespace normalisation; no italic underscores in span. Chose v.215 ('From love comes grief...') over the parallel vv.212-216 (pleasure/affection/lust/greed) and over the v.218 'desire for the Ineffable (Nirvana)' line and the v.154 'extinction of all desires' line: 215 is the hottest and most universal to a stranger, and the love-form bites hardest. The cited locator was 215-216; I kept 215 alone for the cleanest jolt (216 just swaps 'love' for 'greed'). Confidence high.
